q: Chemoreceptors regulate ventilation rate. Which change stimulates an increase in breathing?
options: Decreased blood CO2 (hypocapnia) and increased blood O2
Rising blood pH (alkalosis) detected by aortic body chemoreceptors
Increased blood CO2 causing decreased pH, detected by central and peripheral chemoreceptors
Elevated blood glucose concentration detected by the hypothalamus
explanation: Central chemoreceptors in the medulla are sensitive to H+ (which rises when CO2 increases); rising CO2/falling pH stimulates the respiratory center to increase ventilation.
